headline: Laure Ferrari, the Frenchwoman whispering in Nigel Farage's ear
description: Little-known in France, Ferrari works as an adviser to her partner Nigel Farage, leader of Reform UK, currently leading the polls in the UK.

headline: Russia stands by Mali's military junta at all costs
Trump says cutting US troop numbers in Germany 'further than 5,000'
Gulf monarchies divided amid threats of new US strikes on Iran
description: During a visit to the Gulf countries, French Foreign Minister Jean-Noël Barrot spoke to the divided allies, who seek to achieve greater autonomy over their own defense while avoiding upsetting Washington.

headline: Greek government faces criticism after Israeli attack on Gaza humanitarian flotilla
description: Greek authorities have come under sharp criticism after Israeli forces intercepted a humanitarian flotilla bound for Gaza, in a maritime area under Greek responsibility for search and rescue operations.

headline: Dries Van Noten's Venice palazzo showcases artworks and crafts from around the world
description: After a career in fashion, Dries Van Noten has opened a foundation in Venice's Palazzo Pisani Moretta with partner Patrick Vangheluwe to showcase artists and artisans they admire. The inaugural 200-piece exhibition runs through October, after which the venue closes for several months of renovations.

headline: The Eames House, an enduring utopia spared by the Los Angeles fires
description: In 1949, design icons Ray and Charles Eames built a villa-workshop in Los Angeles that laid the foundation for a new kind of architecture. The landmark building miraculously survived the massive January 2025 fire and was recently celebrated at Milan's Design Week.

The Lebrun brothers and the French team's dream of a first world table tennis title is 'in the back of their minds'
description: With five players ranked in the world Top 30, the French national table tennis team is once again a strong contender for a medal at the World Team Championships, the final stage of which is taking place from May 2 to May 10 in London.

headline: French authors face a reckoning
description: Nearly 200 writers have expressed their desire to leave the Grasset publishing house since the dismissal of its chief, Olivier Nora. This mass departure has highlighted their dependence on the publisher, especially regarding rights.

headline: The labor market is sidelining older workers in Europe and in the United States
description: Older workers face employment challenges due to both discrimination and the economic calculations of recruiters, according to a report from the research and statistics office of the French Ministry of Labor (DARES).
